title: Cloudflare lays off 1100+ amid AI restructuring
summary: Cloudflare announced on May 7, 2026, that it is laying off over 1,100 employees globally, citing massive internal AI adoption as the primary driver for organizational restructuring. This marks one of the most explicit links between AI expansion and large-scale workforce reduction at a major internet infrastructure company, signaling a broader industry trend where AI automation displaces human roles. The severance package includes full base salary through end of 2026, U.S. health insurance coverage through year-end, and extended equity vesting until August 15, 2026, with cliff period waived for employees under one year tenure.
context: Cloudflare is a major content delivery network and internet security company. The company reported a 600% increase in internal AI usage across departments like engineering, HR, finance, and marketing in the past three months, with employees using AI agents for daily tasks. Enterprise AI agents are autonomous systems that can perceive environments, retain memory, and make independent decisions, increasingly adopted across industries.

references.title: AI agents increasingly viable for enterprise use | TechTarget
